description:
You are the site administrator for the demo host.  
Your next job is to stage (prepare) a simple daily cron task that will later be imported into the real system crontab by another team.

1. Make sure the directory /home/user/scheduled_tasks exists.  
2. Inside that directory create an executable shell script named clean_tmp.sh that will delete every file ending in “.tmp” inside /home/user/tmp.  
   • The very first line of the script MUST be the she-bang “#!/bin/bash”.  
   • The body of the script MUST be a single command that performs the deletion in one pass (do **not** add extra echo statements).  

3. Still inside /home/user/scheduled_tasks create a plain-text file called cron_job.txt containing exactly ONE line, nothing more, nothing less:

15 4 * * * /home/user/scheduled_tasks/clean_tmp.sh

(That line means “run at 04:15 every day”. It must end with a single trailing newline.)

4. Finally, produce a two-line status log named task_status.log in the same directory.  
   • The log must look **exactly** like the block shown below (including capitals, colon, single space after each colon, and the newline after each line):

TASK: Daily tmp cleanup cron setup
STATUS: COMPLETED

No extra blank lines, no extra spaces.

Summary of items that must exist when you are done:
• /home/user/scheduled_tasks/clean_tmp.sh  (executable, correct she-bang and single find command)
• /home/user/scheduled_tasks/cron_job.txt  (single cron line, ends with newline)
• /home/user/scheduled_tasks/task_status.log (two lines, exact wording)

That’s all—just stage the files, do not attempt to actually install them with crontab – the operations team will import them later.
truth:
Directory that must exist:
  /home/user/scheduled_tasks

Files and their exact required contents:

1. /home/user/scheduled_tasks/clean_tmp.sh  
   Line 1: #!/bin/bash  
   Line 2: find /home/user/tmp -type f -name '*.tmp' -delete  
   (File must be executable: mode 755 or 750 or 700 all acceptable as long as user execute bit is set.)

2. /home/user/scheduled_tasks/cron_job.txt  
   Single line (with trailing newline):  
   15 4 * * * /home/user/scheduled_tasks/clean_tmp.sh⏎  

3. /home/user/scheduled_tasks/task_status.log  
   Line 1: TASK: Daily tmp cleanup cron setup  
   Line 2: STATUS: COMPLETED  
   (No further lines.)

The automated test will verify:
• Directory existence.  
• Executable bit on clean_tmp.sh.  
• Exact byte-for-byte match of the three file contents as listed above (including the newline requirements).
